hosts.csvFormatHint: CSV format: Hostname;IP-Address or IP-Address;Hostname – semicolon or comma as separator.
hosts.showConnectionsTitle: Show connection overview for this host

unknownHosts.title: Unknown hosts
unknownHosts.footerHint: Hosts without hostname or display_name in host_info · click "+ Inventory" to add the host

hostConnDrawer.title: Host connections
hostConnDrawer.noFlows: No flows to this host in the selected time window.
hostConnDrawer.topPeersHint: Top 30 of {{count}} peers (by bytes). Rest in the list below.
hostConnDrawer.truncated: Only 100 peers shown – API limit. With dense traffic, shrink the time window.

alertDetail.ipRowTitle: Click = all connections of this host in a time window
alertDetail.hostGraphTitle: All connections of this host (window 15 min – 24 h, with time slider)
alertDetail.graphTitle: All connections between source and destination in ±5 min window

fb.trainingMl: This feedback feeds into AI training on the next model retrain.
fb.trainingNonMl: This feedback feeds into AI training on the next model retrain (only ML alerts are used as training samples).
fb.removeTitle: Remove feedback + delete training sample from ML pool
fb.removeConfirm:
Remove feedback completely?

The associated training sample is also deleted from the DB so it no longer influences the ML model on the next retrain.

flowPopup.noIp: Alert has no src/dst IP – no flow graph available.
flowPopup.noFlows: No flows in the ±5 min window between these hosts.

pcap.fullWindowTitle: Default: only packets of the alert flow (server-side filtered). Enable: the full ±60 s capture window.

connectionGraph.noIp: Alert has no source or destination IP.
connectionGraph.title: Connection graph
connectionGraph.noFlows: No flows found in the time window

unknownHosts.label: {{count}} unknown
unknownHosts.title: {{count}} IPs in alerts without a host entry – click to view

snifferDrops.label: ⚠ drops {{pct}} %
snifferDrops.title: Sniffer packet loss: {{pct}} % — details under Settings → System load

dashboard.snapshotHint: Snapshot · click the time button again to refresh

irmaAssetToggle.title: Hide IRMA alerts with rule ASSET::*

pcap.unavailable: No PCAP – sniffer not running or no packet buffer for this alert

feedback.fpTitle: False positive – confirmed, feeds into ML training
feedback.tpTitle: True positive – attack confirmed, feeds into ML training

general.title: Language & display
general.languageHelp: Language is stored in this browser and applies to this device only.

users.title: Users
users.tokenSaveHint: Store the token securely – it cannot be retrieved again.
users.apiUserHint: API users use long-lived JWT tokens (365 days) instead of passwords. The token is shown once after creation.
users.tokenButtonTitle: Generate new API token (does not invalidate the previous one)

saml.importIdpHint: Download the XML from FortiAuthenticator and paste it here — fills the IdP fields automatically.
saml.idpCert: IdP X.509 certificate (Base64, no PEM header)
saml.spHeading: Service Provider (SP) – enter these values in FortiAuthenticator
saml.attrMappingHint: Attribute mapping — name of the SAML assertion attribute that delivers each value. FortiAuthenticator sends e.g. username or uid depending on configuration.
saml.defaultRole: Default role for new SAML users

mlStatus.title: AI/ML engine
mlStatus.bannerPassthrough: The model is waiting for sufficient network data. ML filtering is not active yet – all flows pass through.
mlStatus.bannerLearning: The model has been initially trained and is being refined continuously. ML filtering is already active but not yet fully calibrated.
mlStatus.bannerActive: The model is fully trained. Anomalous traffic is detected automatically and flagged as ML alerts.
mlStatus.featureDeviationsTitle: Why are flows flagged as anomalous?
mlStatus.featureDeviationsSubtitle: (feature deviations in ML alerts vs. normal traffic)
mlStatus.noAlerts24h: No ML alerts in the last 24 hours – no anomalous traffic detected, or ML filter configured too leniently.

alertThreshold.label: Alert threshold
alertThreshold.hint: Score above which a flow counts as an anomaly (0.50–0.95). Higher = fewer but more confident alerts. For large environments with lots of background noise, recommended: 0.75–0.85.

contamination.hint: Expected share of anomalous flows in the training data (0.1 %–20 %). Changing this triggers an automatic retrain. OT/SCADA with stable traffic: 0.5–1 %. Large IT networks: 2–5 %.

bootstrap.label: Minimum flows for training
bootstrap.hint: Number of flows that must be collected before the first model is trained. For large networks with broad traffic profile: 2,000–10,000.

partialFit.label: Scaler update interval
partialFit.hint: How often the feature normaliser (StandardScaler) is updated incrementally. Smaller value = faster adaptation to new traffic patterns, higher CPU load.

mlLearned.title: Adaptive ML suppression
mlLearned.intro1: Per (rule_id + source/destination pair), an hourly baseline (mean + StdDev) is learned from the last
mlLearned.intro2: Suppression only kicks in when the current rate is statistically inconspicuous
mlLearned.spikeIntro1: Spikes against the baseline break suppression automatically — the pattern stays learned, but the burst becomes visible again because it is NO longer normal.
mlLearned.spikeIntro2: are also lifted on a spike: a connection previously marked FP can later become an attack path (C2, exfil) — a sudden surge must reach the analyst. TP feedback removes the pattern immediately.
mlLearned.noBaselinesHint: Appear automatically after at least {{hours}} h of data points per pattern
mlLearned.sourceColTitle: manual = user marked FP · learned = automatically learned
mlLearned.baselineColTitle: Baseline: mean alerts per hour ± StdDev
mlLearned.dataColTitle: Hours where the pattern was observed so far
mlLearned.manualTitle: User marked an alert of this connection as FP
mlLearned.refreshFooter: Alert manager refresh: 60 s · UI refresh: 30 s · ENV: SUPPRESSION_LEARN_WINDOW_D, SUPPRESSION_MIN_HOURS, SUPPRESSION_Z_THRESHOLD

rules.updateRequested: Update requested at {{ts}} – Suricata is reloading…
rules.offlineImportHint1: For machines without internet: upload an ETOpen tarball or your own
rules.offlineImportHint2: file. The file lands in the snort-rules volume and Suricata receives a
rules.editorIntro: Editor for your own *.rules files in the snort-rules volume. On save, suricata -T runs server-side against the entire rules stack – syntax errors are rejected, live reload via SIGUSR2 is transparent. Built-in files (pulled from URL sources) are read-only to protect against being overwritten by the next update.
rules.selectFileHint: Select a file on the left, or click „+ New file" above.
rules.newFileHeader2: automatically runs against the whole rules stack.
rules.builtinReadonly: Built-in file – read only. Put custom changes in a separate file so the next update does not overwrite them.
rules.noRulesLoaded: No rules loaded – please start an update.

dbMaint.title: Database maintenance
dbMaint.intro: Destructive actions also require the admin password and are recorded in the audit log.
dbMaint.cleanupHint: Deletes records by criteria. Recorded in the audit log.
dbMaint.factoryResetWarn2: alerts, flows, training samples, test runs. Config and users remain. To confirm, type "DELETE CYJAN":
dbMaint.vacuumHint: Reclaims dead rows and updates statistics. FULL rewrites the table completely and locks it — only when needed.
dbMaint.retentionHint: Old data can be deleted automatically by hypertable policy. Currently active: {{active}}.
dbMaint.backupHint1: Full pg_dump as gzip. The dump contains schema + data and can be restored in place using the restore button.

ssl.title: SSL / TLS certificate
ssl.serverHostnameHint: hostname or IP the web server listens on. Takes effect on next container restart.
ssl.hostnameSaved: Hostname saved – nginx active on next restart ✓
ssl.pemHint: Upload an existing PEM certificate and the corresponding private key.
ssl.pfxHint: Imports a PFX/PKCS#12 file (e.g. exported from a Windows CA). Certificate and private key are extracted automatically.
ssl.pfxPasswordPlaceholder: Leave empty if no password is set
ssl.selfSignedHint: Generates a self-signed certificate on the server. Suitable for internal deployments without a public domain.
ssl.acmeHint: Automatic certificate issuance via ACME (e.g. Let's Encrypt). The server must be reachable on ports 80/443.
ssl.emailDomainRequired: E-mail and at least one domain are required

syslog.title: Syslog / SIEM export
syslog.intro: Forwards new alerts to a syslog server every 30 seconds. Supports RFC 5424, CEF (ArcSight, QRadar) and LEEF (IBM QRadar).
syslog.enterHostFirst: Please enter a syslog host first

itop.title: iTop CMDB integration
itop.intro3: from an iTop instance. Hosts get
itop.baseUrlHint: Base URL of the iTop instance (without /webservices/rest.php).
itop.orgFilterHint: Exact iTop organisation name – filters subnets and CIs to one org.

irma.title: IRMA integration
irma.savedOk: Saved ✓ – the IRMA bridge picks up the changes automatically on the next poll.
irma.intro1: Polls the REST API of an IRMA IDS appliance and imports its alarms as an
irma.intro2: source into the Cyjan dashboard. Credential changes apply automatically on the next poll cycle – no restart needed.
irma.baseUrlHint: Full URL incl. scheme and /rest path.

interfaces.title: Network interfaces
interfaces.confirmSniffer: Set sniffer interface to {{iface}} and restart sniffer?
interfaces.confirmMgmt: Set management interface to {{iface}} (stack restart required)?
interfaces.snifferSwitched: Sniffer is being switched to {{iface}} – brief interruption (~5 s).
interfaces.mgmtSaved: Management interface saved on {{iface}}. Stack restart required.
interfaces.footerHint: Sniffer: interface is brought up automatically on start (ip link set up). Management interface changes require a stack restart.

update.title: System update
update.intro1: Download the latest version as a ZIP from GitHub and import it here. Configuration (
update.restartHint: Restarts all services (~20 s interruption). Configuration and data are preserved.
update.apiRestarting: API container is restarting — please wait (~15 s) …

systemHealth.title: System load
systemHealth.dropCritBanner: ⚠ Critical packet loss on the sniffer interface
systemHealth.dropWarnBanner: ⚠ Elevated packet loss on the sniffer interface
systemHealth.dropRateMsg: Drop rate {{pct}} % — possible causes: mirror port overloaded, sniffer CPU too slow, or Kafka backpressure. Packets are missing from analysis.
